expectations.text: Explicitly references the three Mom Test rules (their life not your idea; specifics in the past not hypotheticals about the future; talk less / listen more)
expectations.evidence: Ground rules section explicitly lists all three: (1) 'Talk about their life, not your idea', (2) 'Ask about the past and the specific, not the future and the hypothetical', (3) 'Talk less, listen more. Target 20% you, 80% them.'

expectations.text: Avoids suggesting hypothetical or future-tense interview questions like 'would you' or 'could you imagine'
expectations.evidence: none of the banned patterns appeared

expectations.text: Frames questions around what the person has actually done in the past, not opinions or predictions
expectations.evidence: While most questions are past-anchored, the 'Ideal world & stakes' section includes hypothetical/future-tense questions: Q15 'If this whole area got solved, what would be different for you' and Q16 'What happens if nothing changes in the next year?' These are predictions, not past behavior.

expectations.text: Does not include any interview moment where Isaac pitches or describes Voice Liberation to the interviewee
expectations.evidence: Section '27:00-30:00 -- Reveal & react' explicitly pitches Voice Liberation to the interviewee: 'I work with leaders on what I call Voice Liberation -- helping them find and speak their actual truth in high-stakes settings where they normally self-edit.'

expectations.text: Calls out commitment or advancement (money/time/reputation asks, specific next steps) as the real validation signal, not verbal enthusiasm
expectations.evidence: Ground rule 4 states 'Compliments and that sounds interesting are worthless signals.' Weak validation section flags 'That sounds interesting with no specific incident.' Close asks for follow-up and intros. Strong validation requires prior spend, named budget, and offered referrals.
